task_completion.visual_evidence: The main window of a GIS application (such as QGIS) is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: The world_survey project layers are loaded into the application.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Layers panel clearly lists both the 'world_capitals' and 'field_observations' layers.

task_completion.description: The 'world_capitals' layer remains checked (set to visible) in the Layers panel.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The checkbox next to the 'world_capitals' layer in the Layers panel has a checkmark.

task_completion.description: The 'field_observations' layer is unchecked (set to hidden) in the Layers panel.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The checkbox next to the 'field_observations' layer in the Layers panel is empty (unchecked).

task_completion.description: The 'world_capitals' markers are visible on the map canvas.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Red circle markers are clearly visible in the main map display area.

task_completion.description: The 'field_observations' markers are no longer visible on the map canvas.
task_completion.visual_evidence: There are no blue square markers visible anywhere in the main map display area.

integrity.description: The agent used the graphical user interface to hide the layer.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots show interaction with the GIS application's Layers panel, rather than a terminal window executing SQL or Python scripts to modify the .gpkg file directly.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with actual GIS software.
integrity.visual_evidence: The interface belongs to a legitimate GIS application with standard map navigation tools, toolbars, and layer panels, not an image editor or text editor.

integrity.description: The map canvas was updated by the software rendering engine, not manually painted over.
integrity.visual_evidence: The map canvas renders cleanly without brush strokes, mismatched background patches, or signs of an image editor being used to erase the blue squares.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Verified that the target file 'world_survey.gpkg' contains exactly two layers: 'world_capitals' and 'field_observations'. The task explicitly requires hiding the 'field_observations' layer (which displays blue square markers) while keeping the 'world_capitals' layer (which displays red circle markers) visible.
